web.markdown:
Doula Services in San Bernardino County \| Birth & Postpartum \| SoCal Doulas

📍 Serving Southern California

# San Bernardino County  _Doula Support_

Expert birth and postpartum doula care across the Inland Empire's largest county — from Ontario and Fontana to Victorville and Big Bear. Covered by IEHP, Kaiser, Medi-Cal, and more.

OntarioFontanaRancho CucamongaSan BernardinoVictorvilleChino

[Check My Insurance — Free](https://www.socaldoulas.com/insurance-verification) [Book a Consultation](https://www.socaldoulas.com/contact)

San Bernardino County families with IEHP, Kaiser, or Medi-Cal often qualify for $0 out-of-pocket doula care. Verify your benefits free.

Other Counties: [Riverside](https://www.socaldoulas.com/riverside-county.html) [San Diego](https://www.socaldoulas.com/san-diego-county.html) [Orange County](https://www.socaldoulas.com/orange-county.html) [Los Angeles](https://www.socaldoulas.com/los-angeles-county.html) [San Francisco](https://www.socaldoulas.com/san-francisco-county.html) [San Bernardino](https://www.socaldoulas.com/san-bernardino-county-doula-service#)

## The Inland Empire's Largest County — _Fully Served_

San Bernardino County is the largest county in the contiguous United States by area — spanning from the Inland Empire cities of Ontario and Fontana all the way to Victorville, Barstow, and the High Desert. It is also one of California's most underserved counties for birth support, which is exactly why SoCal Doulas is here.

Like Riverside County next door, San Bernardino County is predominantly served by IEHP as the Medi-Cal managed care plan — meaning most Medi-Cal families in this county are already covered for doula services. Loma Linda University Medical Center, one of the most respected birth facilities in Southern California, is right here in this county and serves families across the region.

> "San Bernardino County families deserve the same quality of birth support as families in wealthier counties. That's exactly the gap we're here to close — and insurance is usually covering it."

$0

Out-of-pocket for most IEHP & Kaiser families

IEHP

Primary Medi-Cal plan — same as Riverside County

Level IV

Loma Linda NICU — highest level in the region

24/7

On-call doula support during active labor

## Where San Bernardino Families Deliver

* * *

Hospitals We Know

⭐ Regional Center of Excellence

Loma Linda University Medical Center

Loma Linda, CA

One of the most respected academic medical centers in Southern California and the most advanced birth facility in the Inland Empire. Level IV NICU — the highest designation available. Home to exceptional maternal-fetal medicine specialists and a neonatal program affiliated with LLUMC's Children's Hospital. The first choice for complex and high-risk pregnancies throughout both San Bernardino and Riverside counties.

**Doula Tip:** LLUH's scale means your doula's presence is especially valuable for navigating the system. Our doulas are experienced with LLUH protocols and the unique dynamics of academic medical center birth.

Kaiser Contracted

Kaiser Permanente — Fontana Medical Center

Fontana, CA

The primary Kaiser delivery facility for San Bernardino County, serving Fontana, Rancho Cucamonga, Ontario, Rialto, and surrounding areas. SoCal Doulas is a contracted Kaiser provider — Kaiser Medi-Cal and commercial members are eligible for covered doula services. No pre-authorization needed.

**Doula Tip:** Kaiser Fontana is a high-volume facility. Our doulas are experienced supporting families there and know how to advocate effectively within Kaiser's integrated care model.

West Valley

Dignity Health — St. Bernardine Medical Center

San Bernardino, CA

A major community hospital serving the City of San Bernardino and surrounding areas. Strong maternity program with Level II NICU capabilities. Accepts Medi-Cal, IEHP, and a range of commercial insurance plans. One of the primary delivery hospitals for Central San Bernardino County families.

**Doula Tip:** A well-established community hospital with experienced nursing teams. Our doulas know this facility and work well within its care environment.

East Valley

San Antonio Regional Hospital

Upland, CA

Serving the communities of Upland, Montclair, and Claremont in the western San Bernardino Valley. A community-scale hospital with a solid maternity program. Known for a quieter, more personal experience compared to larger regional centers — often preferred by families who want a calmer birth environment.

**Doula Tip:** A great option for West San Bernardino Valley families who want a community-feel birth. Doula-friendly nursing culture.

High Desert

Victor Valley Global Medical Center

Victorville, CA

The primary delivery facility for High Desert families in Victorville, Apple Valley, Hesperia, and surrounding communities. Serves IEHP, Medi-Cal, and other plans common in the High Desert. Our doulas serve High Desert families and are available for births at VVGMC.

**Doula Tip:** High Desert families have fewer local resources — which makes having a knowledgeable doula even more valuable. We serve this area and understand the unique needs of High Desert families.

South Valley

Chino Valley Medical Center

Chino, CA

The delivery hospital for Chino, Chino Hills, Montclair, and Ontario-area families who prefer a more local option. Part of the Prime Healthcare network. Accepts IEHP, Medi-Cal, and major insurance plans. Smaller scale with a more personal feel for families in the South San Bernardino Valley.

**Doula Tip:** A solid community option for the South Valley. Families in Chino and Chino Hills appreciate the proximity and the more personalized experience.

## Insurance Coverage

* * *

Covered Plans

🏥

IEHP — Inland Empire Health Plan

Primary Medi-Cal · Riverside & San Bernardino

IEHP covers both Riverside and San Bernardino County Medi-Cal members. Full California doula benefit — up to 8 visits, continuous labor support, and 2-year postpartum case management. SoCal Doulas bills directly.

[Learn about IEHP coverage →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/iehp)

🔵

Kaiser Permanente Fontana

HMO · San Bernardino County

Kaiser Fontana Medical Center is the primary Kaiser delivery facility for San Bernardino County. Medi-Cal and commercial Kaiser members eligible. SoCal Doulas is contracted — no prior auth needed.

[Learn about Kaiser coverage →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/kaiser-permanente)

🌿

Medi-Cal California

Statewide · No Referral Required

Statewide Medi-Cal doula benefit covers all eligible members throughout pregnancy and up to 12 months postpartum. No referral needed. We handle all billing.

[Learn about Medi-Cal →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/medi-cal)

💙

Health Net

Medi-Cal & Commercial

Health Net Medi-Cal and commercial members throughout San Bernardino County. Includes the Mahmee digital maternity platform option where eligible.

[Learn about Health Net →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/health-net)

🥕

Carrot Fertility

Employer Benefit

Many Inland Empire employers offer Carrot Fertility as a workplace benefit. If your employer is enrolled, doula care may be covered through your Carrot benefits wallet.

[Learn about Carrot →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/carrot-fertility-1)

🔍

Not Sure What You Have?

Free Verification · 1 Business Day

Submit our free verification form and we'll check your San Bernardino County plan directly. We contact the plan for you — no commitment required.

[Check My Benefits Free →](https://www.socaldoulas.com/insurance-verification)

## Communities We Serve

* * *

San Bernardino County

### From the Valley Floor to the High Desert

We serve families across all of San Bernardino County — from the western Valley communities through the High Desert and beyond.

OntarioFontanaRancho CucamongaSan BernardinoRialtoColtonChinoChino HillsUplandMontclairLoma LindaRedlandsVictorvilleApple ValleyHesperia

## How It Works

* * *

Getting Started

1

### Verify Your Benefits

Complete our free form. We contact IEHP, Kaiser, or your San Bernardino County plan directly and confirm your coverage — usually within one business day.

2

### Meet Your Doula

We match you with a SoCal Doulas professional who knows your delivery hospital. You meet, ask questions, and confirm the fit — no obligation.

3

### We Handle Everything

Billing goes directly to your plan. Your doula attends prenatal visits, your birth, and in-home postpartum visits. We handle all the paperwork so you don't have to.

## San Bernardino County FAQs

* * *

Common Questions

Does IEHP cover doula services in San Bernardino County? ▾

Yes — IEHP covers the full California Medi-Cal doula benefit for members in both Riverside and San Bernardino County. Coverage includes an initial visit, up to 8 prenatal and postpartum visits, continuous labor support, and 2 extended postpartum visits. SoCal Doulas is a contracted IEHP provider and handles all billing directly — most IEHP members pay $0.

Why do so many San Bernardino County families choose Loma Linda University for birth? ▾

Loma Linda University Medical Center has a Level IV NICU — the highest designation available — and one of the most respected maternal-fetal medicine programs in Southern California. Families with complex pregnancies, high-risk situations, or who simply want the most comprehensive care in the region frequently choose LLUH. Our doulas are experienced with LLUH's protocols and provide strong advocacy within this academic medical center environment.

Does SoCal Doulas serve the High Desert (Victorville, Apple Valley, Hesperia)? ▾

Yes. The High Desert is one of the most underserved areas in San Bernardino County for birth support resources. We serve High Desert families and can attend births at Victor Valley Global Medical Center in Victorville. High Desert families with IEHP or Medi-Cal coverage are eligible for the same covered doula services as Valley families. Please reach out to discuss your location and confirm doula availability.

I have Kaiser Fontana — am I covered for doula services? ▾

Most likely yes. Kaiser Fontana is the primary Kaiser delivery facility for San Bernardino County. SoCal Doulas is a contracted Kaiser provider, and both Medi-Cal and commercial Kaiser members may qualify for covered doula services. Kaiser members are self-referred — no pre-authorization needed. Submit our free verification form and we'll confirm your specific coverage.

## San Bernardino County's Doula Team

Most families with IEHP, Kaiser, or Medi-Cal qualify for doula care at $0 out-of-pocket. Free verification, no commitment.

[Check My San Bernardino Benefits](https://www.socaldoulas.com/insurance-verification)

**SoCal Doulas** · Birth & Postpartum Doula Support · San Bernardino County, California

951-481-1715 · village@socaldoulas.com · SoCalDoulas.com

­

Happy Mama Healthy Baby Alliance

[Please Donate](https://givebutter.com/hmhba-doulas)[Become a Volunteer](https://form.jotform.com/hmhba/educator-application)

![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/cropped-Pregnant-African-queen.jpg)

# Postpartum Doula Services

_Mothering the Mother in Los Angeles, San Bernardino and San Diego Counties_

![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/HelunaHealth_Tagline_Horizontal.png)

![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/Doula-Breastfeeding-Latina-1024x683.jpg)

# Our Community Partners

# ![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Molina-Logo.jpeg)

# ![kaiser permanente medi-cal](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/kaiser-permanente-medi-cal.png)

# ![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/LA-Care-2-logo-1.png)

* * *

**TO ENROLL OR GET INFORMATION,** **PLEASE FILL OUT THE FORM:**

**[Parent Request For Doula  Services](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/registration-form-for-childbirth-support-services/)**

**Call us at (626) 388-2191 ext. 1 \| Email us at: [doula@motherbabysupport.net](mailto:doula@motherbabysupport.net)**

[**MEET OUR DOULAS**](https://motherbabysupport.net/hmhba-doulas/)

* * *

**PAPA JOSEPH’S EXPERIENCE WITH HAPPY MAMA HEALTHY BABY ALLIANCE’S POSTPARTUM DOULAS**

* * *

**THE ROLE OF THE POSTPARTUM DOULA**

Postpartum Doulas care for new mothers and newborns immediately after birth up to 3 months postpartum. They provide non-medical support for newborns and mothers, including emotional support, household assistance, newborn care, breastfeeding support, and promoting newborn and parental attachment and bonding.![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/Asian-Mom-Baby-scaled.jpg)

The Postpartum Doula may also help with grocery shopping, meal planning and preparation and household duties such as baby laundry or nursery setup. We are not nannies or housekeepers. If you need extended care beyond 3 months or are looking for someone to do deep housecleaning or nannying, please go to [https://care.com](https://care.com/).

Postpartum Doulas are not medical or midwifery providers and do not perform any clinical skills such as newborn or maternal medical exams. We do not make any medical decisions. We work cooperatively with your pediatric medical provider.

The Postpartum Doula’s role is to empower, educate, nurture new mothers and newborns so they can bond and rest and recover after childbirth and adjust to parenthood during the first two or three months.

**ABOUT OUR SERVICES**

- Our organization has been providing free and low-cost Doula support in Los Angeles County since 2010.
- Our [founder](https://motherbabysupport.net/team/cordelia-s-hanna/) established the very first community-based Doula program in Los Angeles County at Pasadena Public Health Department Black Infant Health Program which she ran from 2002-2012.
- We provide new graduate Doulas with the opportunity to gain birth and postpartum experiences that are required for certification while serving low-income families.

**WHO IS ELIGIBLE?**

- Any family in Los Angeles County is welcome.   Starting in mid-2026, we will offer services in San Diego County and San Bernardino County.![Belly Kiss](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/LesbianMamasBellyKiss-800px-1024x557.jpg)
- We serve families of all ethnic backgrounds, gender identities, sexual orientations, and socioeconomic levels.
- Families using a gestational surrogate or adoptive mother are welcome.
- LBGTQIA+ families are welcome. We are a queer and trans safe space.
- We work on a sliding scale or provide volunteer services because we believe EVERY family deserves a Doula. If you do not have Medi-Cal and are low-income, please reach out to us!
- Families who are low-income but do not have Medi-Cal are eligible for volunteer Postpartum Doula Support or reduced fees.  Volunteer services may be offered by a Doula Intern under supervision or a group of Doulas.

**ABOUT OUR DOULAS**

- We are a diverse team of Doulas who come from varied cultural backgrounds.   We are Black, Latinx, Caucasian, Immigrants  and LBGTQIA+ . We serve people from the communities we come from. Our ages range from 21-64. We speak Spanish, French, and African Languages.
- We are here to help all families have stress-free and joyous postpartum experiences and to promote bonding with the newborn.
- Our doulas’ experience levels range from new grads to seasoned Doulas.
- In addition, our  Doulas have a variety of educational backgrounds and certifications in childbirth education, lactation counseling, yoga, placenta encapsulation,  Reiki energy healing, massage therapy, social work, public health, and other disciplines.

**WHAT POSTPARTUM DOULAS OFFER**

_**Emotional Support**![](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Tai-postpartum-visit-1024x576.png)_

- Encouragement
- Reassurance
- Empathy
- Praise
- Affirmation
- Support for new father/partners.

_**Physical Comfort**_

- Suggest methods for healing of perineum
- Suggest comfort measures for postpartum Afterpains, sweating, breast soreness, etc.
- Provide mother with support for breastfeeding the baby; we help with common problems such as low milk supply, sore nipples, engorgement, etc. We can make referrals to IBCLCs for  help with more complex challenges if needed,
- Massage mother and/or teach infant massage
- Utilize aromatherapy for relaxation and stress reduction.

**_Advocacy & Education_**

- Help client communicate with doctors and nurses about newborn care.![Happy Multicultural New Family](https://motherbabysupport.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/02/Happy-Multicultural-New-Family-270.jpg)
- Educates about risks and benefits of newborn procedures (circumcision, Newborn Screen Test, etc.)
- Teach how to manage breastfeeding
- Teach how to  diaper, swaddle, soothe, and bathe newborn
- Educate on how to know if baby is getting enough breastmilk
- Explain what the doctor, midwife or nurse wants to do and why
- Inform clients of risks and benefits of  newborn medical decisions
- Encourages informed decision-making regarding their baby’s care
- Encourages Informed Consent and Refusal when making medical decisions
- Help the client and her partner decide what they want to do
- Provides language interpretation if needed
- Encourages Client’s Religious/Spiritual Practices
- Advocates for Respect of Client’s Cultural Traditions
- Protects Client Privacy and Intimacy
- Ensure each mamababy is treated with kindness, dignity and respect

**_Recognize & Refer_**

Doula will educate client and refer to primary medical provider if there are:

- Danger signs of postpartum parent
- Danger signs in the newborn
- Complex issues of lactation

* * *

**FEE FOR (NON-MEDI-CAL) SERVICES**

Professional Postpartum Doula services are available for **half-day**, **full-day,** **overnight** and **weekly** and **monthly** rates.

Pay For Postpartum Doula Support

**Postpartum Doula Support Rates**

**Postpartum Doula Support is offered on an half-day, full-day, weekly, or monthly rate. Postpartum Doula support can begin as early as 72-hours after the birth through 8-weeks postpartum.**

- Half-Day Rate (4 hours): $200
- Full-Day Rate (8 hours): $400
- Overnight Rate (8 hours): $500 (10:00 pm to 6:00 am OR 11:00 pm to 7:00 am)
- Weekly Rate: $1,800 (8 hours per day for 7 days)
- Monthly Rate (24/7): $9,500 (7 days/week, 24 hours/day for 30 days)

**Choose your own price from the dropdown menu!**

Processing _._ _._ _._

Pay Now By Card

**Pay By Check**

Make checks payable to _“HMHBA”_. Send checks to:

Happy Mama Healthy Baby Alliance

600 Lincoln Ave., Unit #92495

Pasadena, CA 91109

[**PAY FOR BIRTH DOULA SERVICES HERE**](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/pay-for-parent-support-services/)

**OTHER SERVICES OFFERED**

[**Placenta Encapsulation Services**](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/placenta-encapsulation-services/)

Thinking about placentophagy (eating your placenta)? We offer placenta encapsulation services. Pick up and delivery are available. [Learn more about Placentophagy here](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/placenta-encapsulation-services/).

[**Postpartum & Breastfeeding Education Classes**](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/breastfeeding-education/)

These classes will educate new mothers about newborn care, breastfeeding concerns, and postpartum recovery, including postpartum mood and anxiety disorders,.The classes are included in the _**Nurturing Roots Postpartum Doula Service**_ free of charge.

**Classes and Workshops**

_All our events and classes are occurring on Zoom. We offer:_

[Breastfeeding Education and Consultation](https://motherbabysupport.net/projects-programs/breastfeeding-peer-counselor-program/)

[Pre and Postnatal Yoga Classes](https://motherbabysupport.net/parent-support/prenatal-and-postpartum-yoga/)

* * *

**FOR MORE INFORMATION CONTACT US!**

**Call: (626) 388-2191 ext. 1 \| [Email us](mailto:doula@motherbabysupport.net)**

Every Dollar Helps

© Copyright 2010-2026 [Happy Mama Healthy Baby Alliance](https://motherbabysupport.net/) All Rights Reserved.

# Welcome to

# Moon Sister Doula Care

## [Copy heading link](https://www.moonsisterdoulacare.com/\#h.1aeewnddnc71)    _**My mission is to serve you emotionally, mentally, physically, and spiritually so that you feel confident, informed, supported, and nourished in your postpartum experience.**_

## [Copy heading link](https://www.moonsisterdoulacare.com/\#h.rtossnbrmpel)    As a postpartum doula, I am ready to gently guide and aid you through the after-birth phase of your life. Wherever your life currently stands, whether waxing or waning, full or new, I am by your side to ease you into the momentous experience that is postpartum.  I offer you my compassion, connection, support, resources, skills, and respect.

## [Copy heading link](https://www.moonsisterdoulacare.com/\#h.vg91p71w9ezj)    I am passionate about supporting you on every level - physically aiding you, mentally providing you with confidence, emotionally uplifting you, and spiritually rejoicing with you. My objective is to help with _your_ needs and make you feel comfortable in _your_choices.

To both new and experienced parents, I welcome you to explore some of the numerous benefits of a postpartum doula:

- Improved infant and maternal health (directly lowers rates of mortality for both)

- Early detection and decreased rate of Postpartum Depression (if it does occur, it is shorter duration)

- Earlier bonding between infant and parent(s)

- More successful nursing (earlier problem solving and overall longer duration)

- Greater confidence and less anxiety surrounding parenting

- Decreased parental exhaustion, frustration, and burn out

- Decreased rates of infant dehydration and hospitalization

- Fewer unnecessary calls to the pediatrician or other care providers

- Earlier return to work with less anxiety for partners

- Greater control in the face of overbearing relatives

web.markdown:
top of page

# Free & Cost-Effective Services

#### A compilation of free and cost-effective resources from local organizations.  These resources are available to individuals and families in San Bernardino County who are at various stages of their pregnancy journey.

![they-cant-resist-this-little-one-shot-of-a-young-2023-11-27-05-00-09-utc.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_229c57b06f8f4ee4b87104cd10e9dad4~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_953,h_431,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/48bf12_229c57b06f8f4ee4b87104cd10e9dad4~mv2.jpg)

Filter programs by clicking on the categories below:

Baby ItemsBaby SafetyBlack/African AmericanBreastfeeding/LactationChild Support ServicesChildbirth EducationChildhood DevelopmentClinicsClothingDental/Oral HealthDoula ServicesEarly Childhood ServicesEmployment/Job SearchFatherhoodFinancial SupportFood & NutritionHome VisitingHotline/WarmlineMental HealthMobile ClinicsParenting ClassesPerinatal LossPerinatal ServicesPostpartum ServicesSTI Testing/TreatmentSmoking CessationSpanish SpeakingSubstance UseSupport GroupsUnhoused & Homeless ServicesWomen's Health

## Click on the thumbnails, to download the flyers below.

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/2ca761_46e25ac067394569931c342a3c607e30~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/2ca761_ab33d36fb9de43b8bb119175a01a7c5f.pdf)

# 24/7 Dad Workshop from SB Fatherhood

Developed by parenting and fatherhood experts, SB Fatherhood's 24:7 Dad® program teaches men the characteristics they need to be good fathers 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Used by a wide variety of organizations across the country, research shows that 24:7 Dad® successfully changes fathers’ attitudes, knowledge, and skills.

[Referral Link](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdgg69Jy6BxVlMwkKhz2mZoGcTrm50HDR8FTT4byVc0fe79eA/viewform)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://sbfatherhood.com/education)

[![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)](mailto:info@sbfatherhood.com)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:19095679508)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/2ca761_5086c3cd6b0d43e48bea8f49a67638a1~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/2ca761_b2ff6d333af44939b5827bc558d3abe4.pdf)

# Baby Steps Program

Hosted by the Sickle Cell Disease Foundation, the BabySteps Program supports parents of children with sickle cell disease by offering healthcare navigation, SCD education, mental health services, community health worker support, and more.

[Referral Link](https://www.scdfc.org/programs-services)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://www.scdfc.org/programs-services)

[![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)](mailto:deborahg@scdfc.org)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:9097435226)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_219f17683e214028bd2bd13be267c8f2~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/48bf12_7d73786e5ee94504915a208bbda909c7.pdf)

# Black Infant Health (BIH) Program - San Bernardino County

Within a culturally affirming environment and honoring the unique history of African American women, the BIH Program offers participants opportunities to participate in weekly group sessions, supported by one-to-one case management, designed to help access their own strengths and set health-promoting goals for themselves and their babies.

[Referral Link](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=U545MZNqqkmMrskp-dSpHUchoGyCa3tKjog1FepKJgxUM1RUUjZBQk9ET0NZOVdMV0o5MU1IS042Ti4u)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://dph.sbcounty.gov/bih/)

[![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)](mailto:BIH@dph.sbcounty.gov)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:18443523985)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/2ca761_32a047d4cc53406c980cc03ae46701a0~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/2ca761_5b2deffbd7314bd6a5f4046f00dca157.pdf)

# Boot Camp for New Dads from SB Fatherhood

This is a three-hour workshop covering fatherhood and the needs of new mothers and new babies. During the workshop, the Coach (facilitator) will make sure new Dads (Rookies) talk about what they are most concerned about as new dads and other important topics, such as: Supporting new moms, breastfeeding, feeding, changing a diaper, how to manage family and visitors, baby’s safety, and more.

[Referral Link](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdgg69Jy6BxVlMwkKhz2mZoGcTrm50HDR8FTT4byVc0fe79eA/viewform)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://sbfatherhood.com/education)

[![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)](mailto:info@sbfatherhood.com)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:19095679508)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_36947f5c0fe54068b48cccc418424cd1~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/48bf12_4247718f46f14a7dbb0568dd08989244.pdf)

# Building Early Education Strategies Program for Parents

Child Care Resource Center’s (CCRC) Building Early Education Strategies program connects families with mental health consultants to build effective, lasting strategies for early childhood development. Participants receive professional consultation, personalized feedback, and non-judgmental support tailored to their needs.

[Referral Link](https://ccrc.jotform.com/211756162121042)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://www.ccrcca.org/providers/building-early-education-strategies/)

[![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)](mailto:RWilliams@ccrcca.org)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:661-450-2430)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/2ca761_5832c523f3dc4a30beb31ff43d11d988~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/2ca761_5ca785246d154c019515887e830eaca5.pdf)

# California Home Visiting Program

Child Care Resource Center's (CCRC) Home Visiting program connects expectant parents and families with children 0-5 years old with a supportive educator who shares helpful tools, resources and is there to celebrate a child’s development every step of the way. Services are available in English & Spanish. Click on the flyer for both versions.

[Referral Link](https://ccrc2.jotform.com/240884841902057)

[![Website Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_8cab2e1a4c6c458c9bfc27298d9783c3~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Website%20Icon.png)](https://www.ccrcca.org/parents/home-visitation/)

![Email Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_2bdfc80c24514ad0ba214345c5160f6e~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Email%20Icon.png)

[![Phone Icon.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_1823c22cf4284955ae191fe69a33b0dd~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_25,h_27,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Phone%20Icon.png)](tel:818-717-1000)

[![Community Supports Thumbnails (1).png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/48bf12_b809b821fa40439da34f01fcb438d19a~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_192,h_249,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Community%20Supports%20Thumbnails%20(1).png)](https://www.maternalhealthnetworksb.com/_files/ugd/48bf12_bfe72ffce5ef48cfb73668239aec444b.pdf)

# Cherry Blossom Family Program

The University of California Health Milk Bank created the Cherry Blossom Family Program to provide support and empower families to make decisions regarding lactation after the loss of a baby. The grief journey looks different for everyone. Their hope is to fill a much-needed gap in service by providing education, supplies, and support so families can comfortably suppress or pump and donate if they choose.

Project FoodBox proudly offers a Medically Tailored Food Box Program to qualifying Medi-Cal members in the Inland Empire. # Blossoming Beginnings Prenatal Program

Simple Solutions Psychotherapy offers Blossoming Beginnings, a four-week program designed to support expecting parents through pregnancy and beyond. The program helps participants understand pregnancy and body changes, prepare for labor, and navigate postpartum recovery and newborn care. Classes meet once a week for two hours each session. The program is free for those with Medi-Cal insurance.

BrightLife Kids is a CalHOPE program that offers behavioral health coaching to all parents and caregiveers of kids ages 0–12 who live in California. # Butterfly Program

Loma Linda University Health's Butterfly Program is designed to provide support to families who are grieving the loss of their baby through miscarriage, stillbirth, or newborn death. We are aware that this is an emotional time for you and your needs are very special. We offer these pages as a resource to you in hopes that we can be helpful as you walk this difficult journey.

# Child Support Services Resource Room

The Resource Room serves as a central hub where customers can open a case, make payments, search for jobs, and connect with family-focused programs and services. Its launch reflects the County’s commitment to strengthening the financial stability and well-being of parents and children. Services include: Help opening a child support case, Payment processing, Job search and employment support, Connections to family well-being programs

# Early Identification, Intervention Services (EIIS) for Infants and Young Children

EIIS is specifically for infants and young children who may display some type of developmental, behavioral concern or who are at risk for disabilities or special needs. The purpose of this program is to identify mild concerns in order to provide early intervention to the child "at risk". EIIS services are provided through a variety of activities, both clinical and non-clinical, to ensure that these children grow to their greatest potential.

# Free Online Support Groups hosted by Postpartum Support International

PSI offers over 50+ virtual and free support groups. Goups are 90 minutes (1.5 hours) in length. The first ~30 minutes is spent providing information, education, and establishing group guidelines. The next ~60 minutes is “talk time,” in which group members share and talk with each other. Group members must be present for the group guidelines before joining in the discussion or “talk time.” Spanish groups also offered.

# Mothers to Be Tours and Classes at Arrowhead Regional Medical Center

This five-week class will prepare you for the most positive and rewarding birth experience possible. Learn about breathing and relaxation techniques, physical and emotional changes to expect, and types of medications sometimes used in labor. It is recommended that classes be started 10-12 weeks before your due date. Please plan to attend both classes in a session, and bring a pillow and blanket to all sessions. Classes are FREE, you must call 909-580-3348 to register.

# Nurturing Resilience: Therapy for Children 0-5

Child Care Resource Center (CCRC) offers free and low-cost therapy services for children ages birth to 5 in San Bernardino County who have experienced trauma. Services are available by appointment and take place in the family’s home, at CCRC’s San Bernardino Office on Mill Street, or virtually. These trauma-informed sessions aim to support emotional healing and improve behavior in a safe, supportive environment tailored to each child’s unique needs.

# Pamper My Baby Workshop for Parents

The Pamper My Baby program was developed to help parents navigate unchartered medical, social/emotional, physical, and cognitive developmental stages that their child should be achieving. It is an educational workshop to provide support and resources for healthy child development. On the spot access to speech therapist, behavioral therapist, parent advocacy and more, which promotes a child's social/emotional, physical and cognitive growth.

[Referral Link](https://chasing7dreams.org/programs/pamper-my-baby/pamper-my-baby-registration/)

# Planting Prosperity Program

Child Care Resource Center’s Planting Prosperity Program offers free, online trauma-informed care (TIC) training for parents of children ages 0–5. Through six comprehensive modules, parents learn how to build safe, stable, and nurturing environments to support their child’s development and well-being. The program enhances parenting skills, confidence, and understanding of trauma's impact. Mental health consultation is also available, providing families with valuable support at no cost.

# Smoke-Free Household and Parent Program (Smoke-Free HOPE)

Smoke-Free HOPE is an 8-week program designed to help pregnant individuals quit tobacco and nicotine use, delivered by trained community health workers who provide personalized support and assist with accessing resources. The program is open to pregnant individuals who are 18+ years, using any tobacco or nicotine products, and willing to quit during pregnancy. To be eligible, individuals must be receiving prenatal care and reside in SBC.

[Referral Link](http://www.smokefreehope.org/contact/)

# Tangelo’s Food As Medicine Program (IEHP Members)

Healthy food is now a covered benefit for IEHP health plan members. For the first time in California’s healthcare history, eligible Medi-Cal patients can receive 12 weeks of free medically-tailored meals or healthy meal kits delivered directly to their homes. If you have any questions or need more information about the Tangelo program, please reach out to Edith McCauley, Tangelo Community Coordinator, at 951-453-3100.

# The Olive Branch Resource Center: Food Distribution

The Olive Branch Resource Center, located at Sierra High School, offers vital support to the San Bernardino community. Every two weeks on Fridays from 9:00am -- 11:30am. Registration is required via referral link. Follow @sierra\_high\_school and Sierra Suns on social media to stay connected.

The cost of a doula in San Bernardino, CA can vary depending on the specific doula and the services they offer. On average, the cost of a doula in San Bernardino, CA can range from $800 to $2000. It's important to research and interview different doulas to find one that fits your needs and budget.

The cost of a postpartum doula in San Bernardino, CA can vary depending on the specific services provided and the experience of the doula. On average, the cost can range from $25 to $50 per hour. Some doulas may also offer package deals for a certain number of hours or days of support. It's best to contact individual doulas in the area to get specific pricing information.

It's important to note that doula fees can vary significantly within the San Bernardino area, with some highly experienced or specialized doulas charging higher rates. Additionally, some doulas offer package deals that may include prenatal visits, continuous labor support, and postpartum support, which can affect the overall cost.


When considering hiring a doula in San Bernardino, it's advisable to research and interview several doulas to find one who aligns with your preferences, needs, and budget. During consultations, you can discuss their specific services and pricing structure to ensure you have a clear understanding of what is included and the associated costs. This process will help you find a doula who meets your needs and fits within your budget.

Hello! I’m Lindsay, a birth and postpartum doula and mom of 2.\\
\\
I became passionate about maternal wellness when I was expecting my first child in Norway. I received such quality care — midwives, visiting baby nurses, cushy parental leave! — but I was still missing the emotional support and expertise I needed to help me navigate a foreign medical system and the many anxieties I held as a first time parent.\\
\\
Since then, I’ve learned from hundreds of parents that they, too, needed more support than was available to them.\\
\\
Often times, they felt others were pushing an agenda that was not in their best interest. I became a doula to help change that, one family at a time.\\
\\
I’m here to hold space for you, learn with you, ease your fears, and empower you to feel confident through this important time in your life.\\
\\
If you are looking for a doula to help you birth and nurture your child on your own terms, please consider booking a free consultation with me. I’d love to learn more about you and see if we are a good fit.\\
\\

Asking Lindsay to be our Doula and specifically assist with postpartum care is the best decision we could have made as parents. Our second born was much more challenging than our first and loved to scream all hours of the day. Not only is Lindsay educated, great at explaining things, and creative, she also has a very patient and calming presence that we desperately needed. SOme of the other Doulas I met were too "out there" and I appreciated Lindsay's "down to earth" responses that were kind and intelligent. She helped us feel confident and stay focused and calm when we thought we couldn't handle the screaming anymore. [![Yolanda Amezcua](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/miwv9ewk51yitf6qun3cg5by14of.png)\\

Riverside, California\\

**Yolanda Amezcua**\\
\\
10 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Yoli 🤍👶\\
A certified doula and experienced Labor & Delivery nurse, and co-founder of Vida Birth Services alongside my business partner, Mercedez.\\
\\
We are proud to be a Latina-owned business based in Riverside, California, serving families throughout the Inland Empire and all surrounding areas. As bilingual providers, we offer support in both English and Spanish to ensure every woman feels understood, respected, and truly cared for—without language barriers.\\
\\
With years of hands-on experience in labor, delivery, and emergency care, I’ve witnessed both the beauty of birth and the gaps that still exist in how women are prepared and supported. We’ve seen too many women walk into birth feeling overwhelmed, unheard, or unsure of their options—and we knew something needed to change.\\
\\
That’s why we created Vida Birth Services 💗\\
\\
Our mission is to empower women through education, advocacy, and personalized support—so they can walk into birth feeling confident, informed, and in control of their experience.\\
\\
At Vida, we offer comprehensive birth education classes designed to teach you exactly what’s happening in your body, what to expect during labor, your options for pain management, and how to recognize and respond to potential complications. We believe that education is power—and when a woman understands her body, she can advocate for herself and make the best decisions for her and her baby.\\
\\
In addition to education, we provide doula support that focuses on emotional reassurance, physical comfort, and strong advocacy during one of the most important moments of your life. From pregnancy through birth, you are never alone—we are there to guide, support, and stand beside you every step of the way.\\
\\
As Latina women in healthcare, this work is deeply personal to us. We are passionate about creating a safe space where women—especially women of color—feel seen, heard, and protected throughout their journey.\\
\\
Every mama deserves to feel heard.\\
Every birth deserves to be respected.\\
And every woman deserves to walk into that room feeling prepared, powerful, and supported.\\
\\
✨ Vida is more than a service—it’s a movement to change the birth experience.](https://www.bornbir.com/yolanda-amezcua)

Hi sweet mama, welcome — and congratulations on your beautiful bundle of joy!\\
\\
My name is Selina, and I am a spirit-filled Christian doula and postpartum doula with over 2 years experience certified through DONA who is deeply passionate about serving and supporting mothers during one of the most important moments of their lives.\\
\\
Pregnancy and birth can bring so many emotions, questions, and unknowns, but you do not have to walk this journey alone. My heart is to help you feel calm, confident, empowered, and fully supported every step of the way—whether you gives birth at home or in the hospital, medicated or unmedicated. \\
I provide emotional, physical, and faith-based support with absolutely no judgment — just compassion, encouragement, and genuine care.\\
\\
I offer comfort measures, breathing techniques, visualization, labor support, spinning babies movements and continuous encouragement to help create a more peaceful and positive birth experience. My goal is for every mama I serve to feel heard, safe, educated, and cared for throughout her journey.\\
\\
During prenatal visits, I focus on education and preparation — I help you understand what to expect, clarify your preferences, and slow things down so you feel empowered and ready. When labor begins, I provide continuous emotional and physical support to help you feel grounded through each stage of the process. In the postpartum period, I meet you wherever you are — Sometimes that means feeding the baby while you pump, teaching you to pump, other times it’s just listening, making a snack, or simply admiring your baby with you.\\
Birth is just the beginning. I’m here to support your whole transition into parenthood — with warmth, wisdom, and deep respect for your journey.\\
\\
Whether this is your first baby or your fifth, you deserve to feel supported, empowered, and loved during your birth experience. I would be honored to walk alongside you during this special season.\\
\\
\\

I didn’t grow up I can say with both parents in my life and even as an adult I don’t have my parents due to there own choices they made it there life . If you are having trouble choosing a doula this is your sign to search no further . I believe with every conviction that mothers hold innate wisdom to birth their babies. , I honor your intuition through holistic care and embodied practice—supporting you through pregnancy, birth, and the sacred postpartum transition.\\
\\
As your Sister in Christ, I offer faith-based birth support for those wanting so. We approach your labor as an act of worship—leaning on prayer and rejoicing to reveal God's strength and love for you.\\
\\
I am a community-first birthworker, passionate about making holistic wellness, coaching, and education accessible to women in my community.\\
\\
My work blends embodied childbirth education and hands-on support across pregnancy, birth, and postpartum. As a Birthing From Within–trained mentor, I offer education that goes far beyond information—our work honors the inner journey, emotional landscape, and transformation that birth invites.\\
\\
Alongside this, I provide holistic, nurturing care including herbal remedies, gentle bodywork, mindful movement, breathwork, and food as medicine—supporting the body’s natural physiology while tending to the nervous system and spirit.\\
\\
My Philosophy: Honoring the MotherBaby Dyad\\
\\
I intimately understand and uphold the crucial MotherBaby bond and believe in family-oriented care that follows natural instincts. This belief shapes every facet of my postpartum support and education:\\
\\
Attachment Parenting: I educate and support families in honoring bonding practices such as exclusive breastfeeding, safe bed-sharing/co-sleeping, elimination communication, and babywearing.\\
\\
Holistic Recovery: I offer nourishing, restorative care in the first 40 days, drawing from my training as a Postpartum Practitioner mentored by Pilar Chandler, Doctor of Ayurveda for Women’s Health.\\
\\
Lactation Philosophy: The Power of Human Milk\\
I am deeply committed to the evidence supporting human milk as the optimal standard for infant nutrition. While I support all feeding choices with heart, my philosophy is rooted in the conviction that exclusive breastfeeding provides unparalleled protection and nourishment. This is not just "good"; scientific consensus confirms that breast is best for both maternal and infant health outcomes.\\
\\

Hello! My name is Annie. I am a DONA certified birth doula with a foundation in Spinning Babies techniques, proudly serving families throughout the SoCal area, with a deep passion for supporting women through one of the most transformative experiences of their lives. As a mother of four, I bring both personal and professional experience to my work, offering compassionate, nonjudgmental support throughout pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum period.\\
\\
My background as a personal trainer with a specialty in perinatal exercise, combined with my work as a health coach, allows me to support my clients holistically—physically, mentally, and emotionally. I believe that preparation and education are powerful tools, and I strive to help every woman feel informed, confident, and connected to her body throughout her journey.\\
\\
I am deeply committed to advocating for the women I serve. My goal is to help you feel seen, heard, and empowered as you make decisions that align with your values and vision for your birth. Whether you desire a calm, supported labor, guidance through the unknown, or reassurance in your strength, I am honored to walk alongside you.\\
\\
Every birth is unique, and it is my mission to help you approach yours feeling confident, focused, educated, and empowered.\\

Recent review\\
\\
Annie was my doula for the birth of my son in December 2024. This was my first baby so I was nervous about what birth would be like and if I could handle it since I wanted to go all natural. The hospital referred us to a list of doulas and after interviewing several, we met Annie. After we hung up the phone, my husband and I immediately said, "This is the one!" \\
Leading up to the delivery day we met with her to discuss our birth plans and what we hoped for. It was a very positive and helpful meeting. She brought up things I wouldn't have thought to consider.\\
When I went into labor, she and my husband were texting and she made her way quickly when we said we were ready for the extra support. She had different tools - tens unit, pressure holds, belly sling, diffuser/oils, and a lot more that I can't recall. Even more than the physical things she brought to support me, her presence was an incredible blessing. She is very good at adapting to the mood of the room. I wanted a quiet, dark environment and her calm, reassuring presence was exactly what I needed. She would suggest trying something new when she could tell I was starting to have a hard time getting through contractions, but would also give me space when I didn't want anyone near me or touching me. As I'd requested during our planning session, she and my husband worked together to support me throughout labor and they would quietly check in with each other about how I was doing and if they thought it was time to try something else. I wanted my husband to be my primary support person and Annie did a great job of coaching him and having a balance between supporting me and giving my husband and I space when I wanted it. They made a great team for me! Lastly, she has endurance! It was a long labor and she was with us for over 10 hours of it. She kept up her quiet, calm energy throughout and continued to support me physically too (like with holding my belly up in the sling or doing pressure squeezes on my forehead) for hours. That must have been tiring but she never gave any indication that it was. I am so grateful for her involvement in the birth of our son.\\
I truly could not have done it without the support of Annie - she was amazing! I absolutely want her there for the birth of our future babies and would recommend her to anyone! \\
\\
\- Madison G.](https://www.bornbir.com/anne-griffith)

With years of experience as a midwife and birth professional, I support families through pregnancy, birth, and postpartum with a holistic, grounded approach that honors both physiology and personal choice.\\
Whether you are planning a home birth, hospital birth, birth center delivery, medicated or unmedicated experience, my role is to help you feel fully prepared — physically, emotionally, and mentally — for the birth you desire.\\
\\
My pregnancy bodywork sessions are designed to support optimal fetal positioning, pelvic balance, and nervous system regulation. When the body is aligned and tension patterns are released, babies can settle into ideal positions and labor often unfolds more smoothly. A regulated nervous system allows you to feel safer, more present, and more empowered — which can significantly impact both birth and postpartum recovery.\\
\\
In addition to hands-on bodywork, I offer comprehensive childbirth education that prepares you for the realities of labor, informed decision-making, partner support, and postpartum recovery. My goal is not to push a specific birth path, but to equip you with knowledge, confidence, and tools so you can navigate your experience feeling supported and in control.\\
\\
I bring both clinical insight and steady presence. I understand the nuances of hospital systems and home birth environments, and I help families advocate for themselves while staying connected to their intuition.\\
Birth is a rite of passage — and preparation matters. When your body is balanced, your nervous system is regulated, and your mind is informed, you enter birth grounded and ready.\\
It is an honor to walk alongside families as they prepare to welcome their babies and step into parenthood with confidence.](https://www.bornbir.com/ana-ruiz)

web.markdown:
# Southern California Doula Services

### Revised 5/15/2026

This is a list of doulas who are contracted by Kaiser Permanente (KP) to provide services to qualifying KP HMO and/or Medi-Cal members. This list is subject to change over time. KP members can reach out to KP Member Services with questions or for additional information: HMO Members: 800-464-4000 (TTY 711) Medi-Cal Members: 855-839-7613 (TTY 711)
